text: Running technique retraining to land softer reduced the risk of knee injury by approximately two-thirds compared with control treadmill running.
Footwear selection (various options) does not significantly influence knee injury risk in runners.
Multicomponent exercise therapy, graduated running programmes, and injury prevention education programmes do not significantly reduce knee injury risk.
Patellofemoral pain in runners can be reduced in the short term through running technique retraining, medial-wedged foot orthoses, multicomponent exercise therapy, or osteopathic manipulation.
Running technique retraining strategies that focus on softer landing are effective for both preventing knee injuries and managing existing patellofemoral pain.
